title: Fixed-Length, Close-Delimited, Absent, and Incomplete Content · Program Defense
summary: A security review compares near-miss parser decisions for fixed-length, close-delimited, absent, and incomplete content and requires the smallest strict bounded fixed-length and close-delimited content reader with changed-input evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for bounded fixed-length and close-delimited content reader. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Stream exactly the validated content length through independent policy limits without allocating the declared size.
Classify EOF before the required octet count as incomplete framing and prevent dispatch or connection reuse.
Stop after exactly the framed content and retain buffered residual bytes for the next message.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Bounded Content Length Reader · changed case · Quiz Bounded Content Length Reader
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz protocol team handles case 9092 in a incident replay lab retaining minimized request-smuggling corpora. Build deterministic Go evidence to separate exact-length, incomplete, and response-only close-delimited content; preserve this operating constraint: no malformed framing may be normalized and forwarded to another recipient; then truncate one content octet and classify incomplete framing without connection reuse. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Stream exactly the validated content length through independent policy limits without allocating the declared size.

Evidence target: Bounded Content Length Reader · changed case · Quiz Bounded Content Length Reader.
